assertions: After `/z-eval-init`, `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` exists beneath the repository root resolved from `$CURSOR_WORKING_DIRECTORY` when set (otherwise the current working directory), and its contents match `plugins/zoto-eval-system/templates/init-config.yml` byte-for-byte.

Standard output contains the absolute filesystem path to `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml`, wording that every supported key ships commented so behaviour matches implicit defaults until overrides are uncommented, an explicit pointer to `plugins/zoto-eval-system/templates/schema/config.schema.json`, and a reminder that `/z-eval-configure` offers guided setup.

The transcript contains no `/z-eval-configure` invocation, no hand-off to the configure skill subagent, and no automatic execution of any other Eval System command (`/z-eval-start`, `/z-eval-create`, `/z-eval-execute`, `/z-eval-judge`, `/z-eval-update`, `/z-eval-compare`, `/z-eval-advise`, `/z-eval-help`, `/z-eval-workflow`, `/z-eval-jump`, `/z-eval-operator`).

primitive_analysis.summary: Cases cover green-field scaffolding from the packaged template, strict refusal when a config already exists, destructive refresh via --force, resolver failures when the template path is absent, and permission errors surfaced verbatim. Each success path asserts the mandated confirmation line and confirms init never delegates into `/z-eval-configure` or other Eval System commands.

cases.expected_output: The command exits successfully after creating `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml`, prints one confirmation line that includes an absolute path to that file, explains defaults stay inactive until uncommented, points maintainers at `plugins/zoto-eval-system/templates/schema/config.schema.json`, and recommends `/z-eval-configure` for guided setup without triggering it.

assertions: After `/z-eval-init`, the process exits with a non-zero status.
Standard error or standard output includes `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml already exists; pass --force to overwrite`.
`workspace/.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` still equals the seeded `# eval-system prior-state marker` content exactly.
